mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market health and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and rising consumer prices indicate stagflationary pressure that erodes corporate profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually while RSAFS declines, or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production above its 12-month moving average confirms strengthening economic output, which drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns during late-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: Positive short-term returns do not materialize when INDPRO crosses above its trend during periods of rising long-term yields, or signal accuracy falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP consistently correlates with higher corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 5-year rolling window or quarterly returns are negative in greater than 55% of positive GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 600,000 units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and related consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in greater than 60% of months where housing starts breach the 600,000 threshold over a 4-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 50% of observed months or shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 2.5% annually forces monetary authorities to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds the 2.5% threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during rate-hike cycles.
